source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Represent and use number bonds and related subtraction facts within 20.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack develops part-whole understanding, systematic bond finding and related subtraction through audio-led, concrete play before symbols.

objective.child_goal: I can split and join numbers to make a whole.
objective.statement: Recall, represent and reason about number bonds and connected subtraction facts within 20.
misconceptions: Counts the same object in both parts or leaves an object out when splitting a whole.
Thinks the parts must stay in one order, so 3 and 7 is seen as different from 7 and 3.
Guesses a missing part without checking that both parts recombine to the whole.
Treats a related subtraction fact as an unrelated new calculation.

vocabulary.visual_anchor: One large garden basket holding every counter.

vocabulary.audio_script: A part is one group inside the whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two small pots receiving counters from one basket.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Two parts that join to make a whole number.
vocabulary.audio_script: A number bond shows the parts that make a whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two root paths joining one flower labelled with the whole.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The part we need to find.
vocabulary.audio_script: The missing part completes the whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One filled pot and one outlined pot beneath a full basket.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Look, then tap how many seeds there are.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate subitising and careful one-to-one counting before splitting sets.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: See the whole group first. You can count if you need to.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Up to ten large seeds arranged in familiar dice and ten-frame patterns.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Move the seeds into two pots. The whole stays the same.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish conservation of the whole while the parts change.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: We can split the seeds in different ways. No seeds appear or disappear.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One basket of seven seeds above two empty pots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Two seeds and three seeds make five seeds.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a number bond with objects, part-whole language and a matching equation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The parts are two and three. The whole is five. Two and three make five.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Five counters split 2 and 3, with a quiet 2 plus 3 equals 5 strip revealed last.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Fill the frame. How many more make ten?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Build fluent complements to ten using an anchored visual structure.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Look at the empty spaces. They show the missing part.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A large ten-frame with counters and tappable empty spaces.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Seven and three make ten. What else do we know?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check commutativity and connection to related subtraction without increasing reading load.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The parts can swap places. If ten loses seven, three is left.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A reversible part-whole mat and one covered part.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show Pip how to find the missing part of eight.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation and reconstruction to confirm part-whole reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I can see five, but the whole is eight. How can I find the hidden part?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Five visible counters, a whole label of eight and a covered second pot.

manipulatives.purpose: Make number bonds tangible by moving one conserved set between two parts and recombining it.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children tap or drag large counters between pots; tap-only, keyboard and switch-step modes perform the same moves.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets, generous drop zones, no time limit, audio count-back, colour-independent patterns and an automatic-placement option reduce motor and visual load.

manipulatives.purpose: Show complements to ten and teen-number bonds through filled and empty spaces.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: A single tap adds or removes a counter; an optional divider marks two parts without requiring precise dragging.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Counters use shape plus colour, frame cells have spoken positions, and reduced-motion mode changes states instantly.

practice.interleaving_notes: Begin with wholes to 5, then bonds to 10, then wholes to 20. Interleave related subtraction only after the child can rebuild the whole reliably.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build every split of wholes to five while preserving one object per count.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: whole 3-5 + one conserved counter set + two large part pots + tap or drag response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check one-to-one audio counting, colour-independent grouping and equivalent tap-only completion.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use visible empty spaces to secure complements and number bonds to ten.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ten-frame with 0-10 filled cells + find or build missing part + spoken prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Randomise spatial patterns only after canonical five-and-ten structures are secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Extend part-whole reasoning to number bonds within twenty using ten-and-some structure.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: whole 11-20 + one full ten + loose ones + missing part target
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep both tens and ones visible; do not introduce formal column methods.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Connect swapped addition bonds and related subtraction facts to the same part-whole model.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: shown bond model + choose matching addition or subtraction fact from three audio-labelled cards
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Read every symbolic statement aloud and avoid relying on operation-symbol recognition alone.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retrieve known bonds in varied object, frame and short story contexts after spaced intervals.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + known whole + picture-supported missing part or related fact choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep stories to one spoken sentence, offer replay and exclude speed scoring for SEND learners.

body.prompt: There are five seeds. Put two in the blue pot. How many go in the other pot?

accept: three counters in the other pot

hints: Keep all five seeds in the two pots.
Count the seeds that are not in the blue pot.
question_variants.explanation: Two and three are the two parts. Together they make the whole, five.

body.prompt: Six spaces are full. How many more counters make ten?

question_variants.explanation: Six counters and four more counters fill the ten-frame, so 6 and 4 make 10.

body.prompt: Eight and two make ten. Tap the take-away fact that matches.

hints: Cover the part with eight. The part with two is left.
question_variants.explanation: The whole is 10. Taking away the part 8 leaves the other part, 2.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts the same object in both parts or leaves an object out when splitting a whole.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The two constructed parts do not recombine to the displayed whole.
repair_sequence: Return every counter to the whole basket.
Move one counter at a time while each move is counted aloud.
Recombine both parts and check the whole before trying a new split.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child makes two different splits of the same whole without losing or double-counting a counter.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks the parts must stay in one order, so 3 and 7 is seen as different from 7 and 3.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child rejects a bond after the two part pots swap positions.
repair_sequence: Build 3 and 7 with two visibly different pots.
Slide the pots to swap places without moving counters between them.
Recount the unchanged whole and name both orders.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child accepts and states a reversed bond for a new whole.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Guesses a missing part without checking that both parts recombine to the whole.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The selected missing part does not complete the frame or whole basket.
repair_sequence: Show the whole and the known part together.
Fill only the visible gaps using one counter per space.
Join both parts and say the complete bond aloud.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child finds a new missing part and verifies it by rebuilding the whole.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats a related subtraction fact as an unrelated new calculation.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child can build an addition bond but cannot identify what remains when one part is covered.
repair_sequence: Build both parts inside the whole.
Cover one complete part without moving the other.
Name the whole, the removed part and the part left.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child selects both related subtraction facts for a new bond model.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain background, muted counter colours, optional sound effects and no confetti or flashing.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace flying counters with instant state changes and a static before-and-after part-whole model.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Speak every instruction, number and equation with replay; audio describes the model without requiring written decoding.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use numerals, counters, frames and speaker icons; written prompts stay to one short sentence.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Present one whole and one action at a time, hide unused controls and use predictable build-check-say routines.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Offer unlimited thinking time, repeatable count audio, a pause after each counter move and worked-example replay.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat incomplete bonds as models to rebuild, preserve the child's placed counters and avoid red failure states.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach whole, part, altogether, missing, more and left with gesture and matched picture models.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate concrete and ten-frame bonds within 10 plus supported bonds within 20; secure requires retained missing-part and related subtraction reasoning across formats after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can split and join numbers within 20, find missing parts and connect a number bond to simple take-away facts.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Builds and explains part-whole models, recalls complements to 10 and uses the same model to derive related subtraction facts without speed pressure.
next_actions: Revisit uncertain complements through a five-minute concrete routine.
Connect secure bonds to one-step addition and subtraction stories.
Continue oral equation language before expecting independent symbol reading.

qa.notes: Deep review sample for the first balanced Year 1 wave. Requires independent teacher review, SEND interaction testing, narration listening approval and pilot calibration before promotion.
